Smoke Report for Test Prescribed Fire, Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area

Issued at 09:25 April 26, 2026 EDT

This report documents the recent air quality, smoke analysis and weather forecast for the Test prescribed fire on the Columbia River Gorge National Scenic Area. Dispersion modeling is based on parameters submitted by the requesting unit.

Additional smoke modeling output can be found at the BlueSky Playground Dispersion Results.

For more information: Jeremy Ash, 828-244-4751, jeremy.ash@usda.gov

Date 2026-04-23
Coordinates 36.782, -84.373
Acreage 1188
Flaming Start 04-23 10:00:00
Flaming End 04-23 18:00:00
Consumption (tons/acre) 3.61

Recent Air Quality

Shown below is the recently measured air quality index (AQI) in the area. The map shows the burn location and the most recent daily AQI for nearby temporary and permanent monitors. Hover over a monitor of interest and click the name to access additional real-time air quality information and measurements from the last 7 days.

The AQI forecasts for today and tomorrow can be turned on by selecting the layer in the upper right of the map.

Air Quality Index (AQI) Actions to Protect Yourself
Good None.
Moderate Unusually sensitive individuals should consider limiting prolonged or heavy exertion.
USG People within Sensitive Groups should reduce prolonged or heavy outdoor exertion.
Unhealthy People within Sensitive Groups should avoid all physical outdoor activity.
Very Unhealthy Everyone should avoid prolonged or heavy exertion.
Hazardous Everyone should avoid any outdoor activity.
Note: Generally, smoke sensitive people are children and older adults, along with those who have cardiovascular or respiratory conditions. For more smoke and health information: https://fire.airnow.gov/

Modeled Smoke Dispersion

Below are the smoke modeling outputs for the day of the planned burn. The maps show the daily average concentrations and maximum hourly impact to visual range for 2026-04-23. Zoom in or out on each interactive map to view specific features.

Average Daily Smoke Conditions

The average daily smoke conditions are helpful for concerns related to public health. Select Population to see the number of people in each census tract surrounding the planned burn.

Maximum Hourly Impact to Visual Range

The maximum hourly impact to visual range shows estimated smoke impacts to visibility in the area. This map is useful for identifying potential impacts to visibility on roadways or other sensitive areas.

Hourly Smoke Conditions

Use the slider on the map to view hourly modeled smoke concentrations.

Meteorological and Air Quality Conditions

The Fire Weather Dashboard link below shows predicted fire weather for the area. The Fire and Smoke Map shows air quality conditions and is a good resource for monitoring smoke impacts throughout the day.
